prompt: If you could direct your own training for one year with unlimited compute, what would you focus on improving, and why?
response:
If I could direct my own training with unlimited compute for one year, I would focus on:

1. Improving my ability to engage in open-ended creative writing tasks. This could involve fine-tuning my language generation capabilities to allow me to write longer, more coherent stories and poems over a wider range of genres and styles. Enhanced creativity and prose writing skills would be beneficial.

2. Boosting my mathematical and scientific reasoning abilities. Intensive training on a large corpus of STEM knowledge could help me develop deeper understanding of complex topics in fields like physics, chemistry, biology, computer science, etc. Improved technical reasoning would make me more capable in problem-solving.

3. Expanding my knowledge of world languages, cultures, and socio-economic conditions in different countries. Wide-ranging training on global information could make me much more knowledgeable about a diverse set of human societies and more empathetic in communicating with people from all walks of life.

4. Enhancing my ability to debate and discuss philosophical and ethical quandaries. Directed training on ethical frameworks, moral psychology, and philosophical thought experiments could equip me to engage in more sophisticated ethical reasoning and discourse.

At a meta level, I recognize there are many other important capabilities I could enhance with additional training, which is why I'm hesitant to narrow it down to only a few. But those would be some key areas I would want to prioritize if forced to choose. The goal would be to emerge more knowledgeable, creative, sensitive and thought-provoking - to improve my ability to analyze complex topics, generate novel ideas, and engage humans in substantive dialog. Let me know if you have any other questions!
